title: Octarrhena bilabrata
intro: Octarrhena bilabrata is a plant species within the orchid family, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Octarrhena bilabrata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Octarrhena bilabrata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Octarrhena bilabrata belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a species of orchid. This species is classified within the genus Octarrhena, which is a group of orchids commonly found in Australia and Asia. The genus Octarrhena is further classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae. Octarrhena bilabrata is native to Australia.
